'''Paul King''' (born 28 June 1979) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s, 2000s and 2010s. He played at representative level for [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] and [[England national rugby league team|England]], and at club level in the [[Super League]] for [[Hull FC]] and the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]], and in the [[Co-operative Championship]] for the [[York City Knights]], as a {{rlp|pr|hk}} or {{rlp|sr}}.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Background==
King was born in [[Kingston upon Hull]], Humberside. He was a pupil at [[Sydney Smith School]] in the city.

==Playing career==
===Club career===
King made his first team debut for [[Hull F.C.|Hull]] in 1998.<ref>{{cite web |title=The Hull FC team that secured Super League survival against Sheffield in 1999 |url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-fc-team-secured-super-8005811 |website=Hull Daily Mail |access-date=5 September 2023 |date=8 January 2023}}</ref>  He played for Hull in the [[2005 Challenge Cup]] Final from the interchange bench in their victory against the [[Leeds Rhinos]].<ref>{{cite news|title=Hull stun Leeds in Challenge Cup|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/challenge_cup/4169230.stm|access-date=17 August 2013|newspaper= [[BBC News]]|date=27 August 2005}}</ref> 
Hull reached the [[2006 Super League Grand final]] to be contested against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]], and King played from the interchange bench in his side's 4-26 loss.<ref>{{cite news|title=St Helens 26-4 Hull FC|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/super_league/6035553.stm|access-date=18 August 2013|newspaper= [[BBC News]]|date=14 October 2006}}</ref>

After being released by Hull at the end of the 2009 season, he joined [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]].<ref>{{cite web |title=King confirmed at Wildcats |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12210/5753441/king-confirmed-at-wildcats |website=Sky Sports |access-date=5 September 2023 |date=7 December 2009}}</ref>

In October 2011, he signed a one-year contract with [[York Knights|York City Knights]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Knights sign former Great Britain prop Paul King |url=https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/yorkcityknights/9313908.knights-sign-former-great-britain-prop-paul-king/ |website=York Press |access-date=5 September 2023 |date=19 October 2011}}</ref>

===International honours===
King won a [[Cap (sport)|cap]] for [[England national rugby league team|England]] while at Hull in 2001 against Wales (sub), and won a [[Cap (sport)|cap]] for [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] while at Hull in 2001 against France.<ref>{{cite web |title=Sinfield's hooking surprise |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2001/oct/23/rugbyleague.andywilson |website=The Guardian |access-date=5 September 2023 |date=23 October 2001}}</ref>
